responses: The general admission process at IILM involves: choosing your program, submitting the application form online or offline, uploading required documents, and completing the admission review.
For BTech, the admission process typically requires 12th mark sheets, identity proof, and completing the IILM application form. Entrance scores may be considered.
For MBA, the admission process generally requires graduation certificates, entrance exam scores (CAT/MAT/XAT if applicable), and completion of the IILM application.
You can apply online through: https://iilm.edu/apply-now/ or contact the admission office for offline admission support.
For exact steps and latest timeline for your specific program, visit: https://iilm.edu/admissions/

responses: Eligibility at IILM depends on your program level and background. For UG programs (BTech, BBA, Law after 12th), you need 10+2 completion. For PG programs (MBA), you need a bachelor's degree.
If you share the specific course you are interested in, I can give you the exact eligibility criteria for that program only.
Common eligibility requirements: 10+2 with relevant subjects for UG, graduation from a recognized university for PG/MBA.
Official eligibility details: https://iilm.edu/admissions/

responses: For BTech at IILM, you need to have completed 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) from a recognized board.
A minimum percentage in PCM is usually required. JEE score may be considered but is not always mandatory — IILM has its own admission process.
If you share your Class 12 PCM percentage and whether you have a JEE score, I can give you a clearer picture of eligibility.
Official engineering admissions page: https://iilm.edu/undergraduate-admissions/

responses: For MBA at IILM, you need a bachelor's degree from a recognized university. Most students apply with CAT, MAT, XAT, or CMAT scores, though IILM may also admit based on academic performance.
Final-year graduation students can also apply provisionally. The exact cutoff and score requirements depend on the current admission cycle.
If you share your graduation background and any entrance exam score, I can tell you if your profile looks strong for MBA admission.
For the latest MBA admission criteria: https://iilm.edu/mba-admissions/

responses: IILM (Indian Institute of Learning & Management) is a UGC-recognized private university offering career-focused programs in Engineering, Management, Law, Liberal Arts, Design & Fashion, and Hospitality.
IILM is known for its industry-linked curriculum, strong placement support, and campuses in Greater Noida and Gurugram.
Students generally find IILM good for MBA, BTech CSE, BBA, and Law. If you want, I can help you evaluate IILM based on a specific course or goal.
For an honest picture, I suggest checking placements data at https://iilm.edu/placements/ and comparing against your career goals.

patterns: I am confused about which course to take
Help me choose between MBA and BTech

Which course should I choose?
Help me decide between courses

Suggest me a course
Which is the best course at IILM?
I want to change my stream
Which course has better scope?
responses: Choosing between courses depends on your career goals, interests, and academic background — not just what others suggest.
If you enjoy technology, problem solving, and engineering: BTech CSE or AI/ML would suit you best. If you prefer business, leadership, and management: MBA or BBA is the right path.
For Law, you need a passion for legal reasoning and argumentation. For Liberal Arts, Design, or Hospitality — these suit creative and communication-oriented students.
Tell me your stream (Science / Commerce / Arts) and what career excites you most, and I can help you pick the best-fit program at IILM.

responses: Unfortunately, most undergraduate programs (BTech, BBA, Law) require you to have passed Class 12 from a recognized board as a basic eligibility condition.
If you have a compartment or are appearing for improvement exams, you may be eligible for provisional admission subject to clearing the exam before the academic year begins.
I strongly recommend contacting the admissions office to explain your situation — they can guide you on the best available options: https://iilm.edu/contact/ or +91-080 6590 5220.

IILM Greater Noida has the following academic leadership:
• Dr. Munish Sabharwal — Prof. & Dean, School of Engineering

• Dr. Arvind Kumar Jain — Prof. & Dean of Student Welfare (additional charge: Dean Research)
• Dr. Babita Singh Parasain — Prof. & Director, School of Law
• Dr. Anupama Srivastava — Prof. & Dean, School of Humanities and Social Sciences
• Dr. Yogesh Kumar Jain — Prof. & Deputy Dean, Executive Education (Management)

Associate Deans at IILM include:
• Dr. Himanshu Sharma — Associate Dean, School of CSE
• Dr. Nitesh Kumar Adichwal — Associate Dean (Accreditation & Ranking), School of Management
• Dr. Aswani RS — Associate Dean
• Dr. Syed Mohd Shahzeb — Associate Dean
• Ms. Ekta Saraswat — Associate Dean

Department of Chemistry: Dr. Arvind Kumar Jain (Dean of Research), Dr. Nidhi Rai, Ms. Shraddha Sharma, Dr. Priyanka Bhatnagar, Dr. Soumya Aggarwal, Dr. Sanjoy Gorai, Dr. Geetika Patel.

Full details: https://iilm.edu/greater-noida/school-of-sciences/

responses:

patterns: Are IILM faculty qualified?
How experienced are IILM professors?
Do IILM faculty have PhDs?
What is the faculty to student ratio at IILM?
How good is the faculty at IILM?
Are the teachers good at IILM?
Faculty experience at IILM
Research by IILM faculty
Do IILM professors do research?
responses: IILM faculty are highly qualified — most hold PhD degrees from reputed institutions in India and abroad, with extensive academic and industry experience. The student-to-faculty ratio is approximately 1:50 to 1:65.

IILM organises Faculty Development Programs (FDPs) throughout the year to keep faculty updated with latest trends in their fields. Faculty are also actively engaged in research, consultancy, and corporate training.

IILM's blended learning pedagogy combines case studies, simulations, role plays, seminars, and practical projects — ensuring students benefit from both theoretical knowledge and real-world application. See: https://iilm.edu/greater-noida/faculty/
